title: Rest Is My Natural State
subtitle: An affirmation for claiming rest as birthright rather than reward or achievement
excerpt: A brief affirmation from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. The statement names rest as the body's natural default, something to return to, not something to earn. The second and third lines name it as permanent: a change that holds.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 30 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

content.text: A brief affirmation from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. Day 30 theme: claim rest as a birthright. The statement names rest as the body's natural state, the condition it returns to, not a reward that must be earned or a result that must be manufactured. The final line names it as permanent, already integrated into how the body moves through the world.
Say it three times, morning or evening. The third line is the one to land on: 'I carry this ease', not 'I hope to keep it.'
Rest is my natural state. My body knows how to do this and does it well. I carry this ease into everything that follows.
Adapted from the Day 30 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
